mission.title: Install and Configure Ollama
mission.description: Ollama is an AI platform designed for serving large language models (LLMs) such as Kimi-K2.5, GLM-5, and others. It is ideal for developers looking to integrate AI capabilities into their applications with minimal setup. Key trade-offs include the need for sufficient computational resources and the complexity of managing multiple models.

steps.title: Install the Ollama CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the Ollama command-line interface, which is necessary for interacting with the Ollama platform.

```bash

curl -fsSL https://ollama.com/install.sh | sh

irm https://ollama.com/install.ps1 | iex

```

> Use this command for Linux and macOS.
> Use this command for Windows.

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This command creates a new Kubernetes cluster with the specified version and node count in your desired region.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create ollama-cluster --cluster-version=1.30 --num-nodes=3 --region=us-central1

```

> Ensure you have gcloud CLI installed and authenticated.

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
This command configures your local kubeconfig to connect to the newly created cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials ollama-cluster --region=us-central1

```

> This command retrieves the cluster credentials.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the nodes in your cluster to ensure they are ready.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see all nodes in 'Ready' status.

steps.title: Deploy Ollama on Kubernetes
steps.description:
This command deploys the Ollama application on your Kubernetes cluster using the provided Helm chart.

```bash

helm repo add ollama https://ollama.com/charts

helm install ollama ollama/ollama --version 0.17.7 --namespace ollama --create-namespace

```

> Add the Ollama Helm repository.
> Install Ollama in the 'ollama' namespace.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Ollama
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Ollama deployment from your Kubernetes cluster. Be aware that this will delete all associated resources and may result in data loss.

```bash

helm uninstall ollama --namespace ollama

kubectl delete namespace ollama

```

> This command removes the Ollama release.
> This command deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Ollama
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up any important data. This process will upgrade your Ollama installation to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl drain <node-name> --ignore-daemonsets

helm upgrade ollama ollama/ollama --version 0.17.7 --namespace ollama

kubectl uncordon <node-name>

kubectl get pods -n ollama

```

> Drain the node to prepare for upgrade.
> Upgrade to the specified version.
> Uncordon the node after the upgrade.
> Verify that all pods are running.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to connect to the server'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This usually indicates that your kubeconfig is not set up correctly or the cluster is down.

**Fix:**
Run 'gcloud container clusters get-credentials ollama-cluster --region=us-central1' to refresh your credentials.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'ImagePullBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the Ollama image cannot be pulled from the registry, possibly due to incorrect image name or tag.

**Fix:**
Check the image name and tag in your deployment configuration.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Pod is in CrashLoopBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This means that the Ollama pod is crashing repeatedly, likely due to misconfiguration or resource limits.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs using 'kubectl logs <pod-name>' to diagnose the issue.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU/Memory'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the cluster does not have enough resources to schedule the Ollama pods.

**Fix:**
Increase the node size or number of nodes in your cluster.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new models and improved API endpoints.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see output similar to 'NAME STATUS ROLES AGE VERSION' with all nodes in 'Ready' state when running 'kubectl get nodes'.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the necessary tools and resources before starting the installation.
